title: Omni-WorldBench: Towards a Comprehensive Interaction-Centric Evaluation for World Models
task: Evaluate interactive response capabilities of world models in 4D settings
key_results: Reveals critical limitations in current world models' ability to reflect action-driven state transitions; evaluates 18 representative models across paradigms
comments: Addresses an important gap by shifting evaluation focus from visual fidelity to causal interaction effects, though practical deployment impact remains unclear.

model_io.model: 18 world models (various paradigms: video generation, 3D reconstruction, 4D)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interactive response is the core capability for 4D world models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; current models show critical limitations in this dimension

title: Speed by Simplicity: A Single-Stream Architecture for Fast Audio-Video Generative Foundation Model
task: Audio-video generation with synchronized speech and human-centric motion
key_results: 5-second 256p video in 2 seconds on H100; 80.0% win rate vs Ovi 1.1; 14.60% WER for speech intelligibility
comments: Single-stream design elegantly simplifies architecture while achieving strong multimodal synchronization; multilingual support is practical asset.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single-stream unified attention can match or exceed multi-stream architectures for synchronized generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves state-of-the-art with simpler design

title: LongCat-Flash-Prover: Advancing Native Formal Reasoning via Agentic Tool-Integrated Reinforcement Learning
task: Native formal reasoning in Lean4 via auto-formalization, sketching, and proving
key_results: 97.1% pass rate on MiniF2F-Test with 72 inference budget; 70.8% on ProverBench; 41.5% on PutnamBench
comments: MoE architecture with hierarchical importance sampling provides stable training for long-horizon formal reasoning; notable improvement over open-weights baselines.

inputs: informal problem statement or formal claim

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decomposing formal reasoning into auto-formalization, sketching, and proving enables more stable learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA with sample-efficient training

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical importance sampling stabilizes MoE training on long-horizon tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HisPO algorithm prevents reward hacking and model divergence

title: Look Where It Matters: High-Resolution Crops Retrieval for Efficient VLMs
task: Resolve accuracy-efficiency trade-off in VLMs by retrieving task-relevant high-resolution crops
key_results: Maintains accuracy while reducing computational cost through selective high-resolution processing
comments: Tool-calling mechanism for crop retrieval is practical; automatic supervised data construction enables scalable training.

model_io.model: AwaRes (VLM with tool-calling for crop retrieval)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Selective high-resolution processing via tool-calling preserves accuracy while improving efficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves accuracy-efficiency trade-off improvements

title: OpenResearcher: A Fully Open Pipeline for Long-Horizon Deep Research Trajectory Synthesis
task: Synthesize long-horizon research trajectories for agent training via offline search-and-browse
key_results: 54.8% on BrowseComp-Plus (+34.0 vs base); 97K trajectories synthesized including 100+ tool-call sequences
comments: Fully offline, reproducible pipeline decouples corpus from trajectory synthesis; practical insights into deep research agent design are valuable.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Offline trajectory synthesis with explicit browser primitives enables stable, reproducible agent training
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves competitive performance without proprietary APIs

title: VideoDetective: Clue Hunting via both Extrinsic Query and Intrinsic Relevance for Long Video Understanding
task: Localize query-relevant segments in long videos for question answering
key_results: Up to 7.5% accuracy improvement on VideoMME-long across multiple MLLMs
comments: Hypothesis-Verification-Refinement loop with visual-temporal affinity graphs is intuitive; approach is agnostic to MLLM choice.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Propagating relevance scores via inter-segment affinity improves long-video understanding over query-only localization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent gains across benchmarks

title: SpatialBoost: Enhancing Visual Representation through Language-Guided Reasoning
task: Enhance spatial awareness of vision encoders via language-guided reasoning
key_results: 3.8% mIoU improvement on ADE20K (55.9 to 59.7) for DINOv3
comments: LLM-based linguistic injection of 3D spatial knowledge is creative; multi-turn CoT reasoning progressively builds hierarchical understanding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Linguistic description of 3D spatial structure can improve vision encoder embeddings for spatial tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA on multiple 3D perception benchmarks

title: Manifold-Aware Exploration for Reinforcement Learning in Video Generation
task: Stabilize GRPO alignment for video generation through manifold-aware exploration
key_results: Consistent gains on HunyuanVideo1.5 across VQ, MQ, TA metrics; superior video quality vs prior GRPO methods
comments: Manifold-aware SDE with logarithmic curvature correction is principled; dual trust region prevents long-horizon drift effectively.

outputs: video samples with improved alignment to rewards

hypotheses.hypothesis: Constraining exploration to valid video data manifold stabilizes GRPO for video generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves better reliability than unconstrained GRPO

title: F4Splat: Feed-Forward Predictive Densification for Feed-Forward 3D Gaussian Splatting
task: Adaptive Gaussian allocation for feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting
key_results: Superior novel-view synthesis vs prior uncalibrated feed-forward methods with significantly fewer Gaussians
comments: Densification-score-guided allocation elegantly addresses redundancy; explicit Gaussian budget control without retraining is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatially adaptive Gaussian allocation reduces redundancy in simple regions and across overlapping views
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves quality gains with fewer Gaussians

title: mSFT: Addressing Dataset Mixtures Overfiting Heterogeneously in Multi-task SFT
task: Optimize multi-task data mixtures to prevent heterogeneous overfitting in SFT
key_results: Consistent improvements across 10 benchmarks and 6 models; can improve performance while lowering FLOPs at low compute budgets
comments: Overfitting-aware search identifies and excludes early-overfitting tasks; simple but effective approach to mixture optimization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Heterogeneous task learning dynamics cause differential overfitting that can be mitigated through selective data exclusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; mSFT outperforms uniform mixture strategies

title: Group3D: MLLM-Driven Semantic Grouping for Open-Vocabulary 3D Object Detection
task: Open-vocabulary 3D object detection via semantic grouping from MLLMs
key_results: SOTA on ScanNet and ARKitScenes; strong generalization in zero-shot scenarios
comments: Integrating semantic constraints during instance merging (not post-hoc) is elegant; taxonomy-aware vocabulary groups prevent over-merging.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic compatibility groups derived from MLLMs can improve geometry-only instance merging in open-vocab detection
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduces over-merging and improves label assignment

title: RoboAlign: Learning Test-Time Reasoning for Language-Action Alignment in Vision-Language-Action Models
task: Improve vision-language-action model performance via test-time reasoning alignment
key_results: 17.5% on LIBERO, 18.9% on CALVIN, 106.6% on real-world tasks vs SFT; achieved with <1% additional data
comments: Bridging modality gap between language and low-level actions via RL is well-motivated; dual-agent architecture (reasoning + summary) adds robustness.

model_io.model: RoboAlign (MLLM + diffusion action head + RL alignment)

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL-based alignment refines zero-shot language-to-action reasoning to improve VLA performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; substantial gains on diverse robotics benchmarks

title: Repurposing Geometric Foundation Models for Multi-view Diffusion
task: Multi-view diffusion via geometrically consistent latent space from foundation models
key_results: Outperforms VAE/RAE latent spaces on 2D/3D metrics; 4.4x training acceleration vs VAE
comments: Repurposing geometric foundation model features as latent space is clever; validates geometric consistency aids NVS generation.

model_io.model: Geometric Latent Diffusion (GLD diffusion in geometric feature space)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometrically consistent latent spaces from foundation models improve multi-view diffusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves competitive results without large-scale text-to-image pretraining

title: BubbleRAG: Evidence-Driven Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Black-Box Knowledge Graphs
task: Retrieve evidence subgraphs from black-box knowledge graphs for RAG via optimal informative subgraph retrieval
key_results: SOTA on multi-hop QA benchmarks; improved F1 and accuracy while remaining plug-and-play
comments: Formulating retrieval as NP-hard Optimal Informative Subgraph Retrieval (OISR) is principled; training-free pipeline is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic anchor grouping and bubble expansion improve both recall and precision over black-box KGs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA on multi-hop benchmarks

title: On the Direction of RLVR Updates for LLM Reasoning: Identification and Exploitation
task: Analyze and exploit the direction of RLVR updates for improved reasoning
key_results: Δlog p more effectively identifies reasoning-critical updates than magnitude metrics; test-time extrapolation improves accuracy without retraining
comments: Signed token-level log probability difference provides cleaner signal than magnitude; practical applications at test/train time.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Direction of RLVR updates (signed Δlog p) is more informative than magnitude for understanding reasoning improvements
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables test-time extrapolation and training-time reweighting gains

title: SEM: Sparse Embedding Modulation for Post-Hoc Debiasing of Vision-Language Models
task: Post-hoc debiasing of vision-language models via sparse latent representation
key_results: Substantial fairness gains across benchmarks; preserves semantic fidelity while removing bias
comments: Operating in SAE latent space provides better disentanglement than dense CLIP embeddings; enables precise non-linear bias removal.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sparse latent representations provide better bias-semantics disentanglement than dense embeddings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves fairness gains without degrading utility

title: REVERE: Reflective Evolving Research Engineer for Scientific Workflows
task: Continuously evolve research coding agent behavior via reflection on global failure patterns
key_results: 4.50% improvement on SUPER, 3.51% on ResearchCodeBench, 4.89% on ScienceAgentBench over expert-crafted instructions
comments: Global training context and reflection-based prompt evolution avoid local overfitting; reusable heuristics distill recurring patterns effectively.

model_io.model: REVERE (reflective agent with multi-field prompt evolution)
outputs: evolved system prompt, task template, and cheatsheet

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reflecting on global training context and distilling heuristics improves research agent generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms fixed expert-crafted instructions

title: PivotRL: High Accuracy Agentic Post-Training at Low Compute Cost
task: Post-training agentic tasks via on-policy RL on existing SFT trajectories at low compute cost
key_results: +4.17% in-domain, +10.04% OOD accuracy over SFT; competitive with E2E RL using 4x fewer rollout turns
comments: Pivot identification via high-variance actions and functional-equivalent rewards efficiently capture RL benefits; practical for production scaling.

model_io.model: PivotRL (local on-policy RL with pivot filtering)
outputs: refined policy with improved OOD generalization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Local on-policy RL on high-variance intermediate turns preserves OOD while improving in-domain accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PivotRL achieves E2E RL benefits at SFT-like cost

title: Insight-V++: Towards Advanced Long-Chain Visual Reasoning with Multimodal Large Language Models
task: Generate long-chain visual reasoning trajectories and train MLLMs with dual-agent architecture and RL refinement
key_results: Significant gains on image/video reasoning benchmarks; ST-GRPO and J-GRPO improve spatial-temporal reasoning
comments: Multi-granularity assessment for autonomous trajectory synthesis is scalable; dual-agent design (reasoning + summary) improves trajectory quality.

model_io.model: Insight-V++ (dual-agent reasoning + summary MLLM with ST-GRPO/J-GRPO)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-agent visual reasoning with RL refinement improves spatial-temporal understanding over supervised learning alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ST-GRPO/J-GRPO enable substantial gains on complex reasoning tasks

title: Scaling DoRA: High-Rank Adaptation via Factored Norms and Fused Kernels
task: Accelerate high-rank DoRA via factored norm computation and fused Triton kernels
key_results: 1.5-2.0x inference speedup, 1.5-1.9x faster gradient computation; up to 7 GB lower peak VRAM vs Hugging Face PEFT
comments: Factored norm eliminates memory-intensive dense product; fused kernels reduce traffic and numerical instability effectively.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Factored norm decomposition enables practical high-rank DoRA on single-GPU setups
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves major speedups with stable numerical precision

title: Generalized Discrete Diffusion from Snapshots
task: Discrete diffusion modeling for large-vocabulary text generation via snapshot-based ELBO
key_results: Outperforms existing discrete diffusion and beats autoregressive models at scale for first time
comments: Snapshot-based ELBO enables efficient training without full noising path; generalization beyond discrete domains promising.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Snapshot-based ELBO enables efficient discrete diffusion training for large vocabularies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms prior discrete and autoregressive baselines

title: The Universal Normal Embedding
task: Align generative and encoding latent spaces via shared Gaussian geometry
key_results: Linear probes in both DDIM and encoder spaces achieve aligned attribute predictions; enables faithful, controllable edits
comments: Universal Normal Embedding hypothesis provides unifying view of generative/encoder latent spaces; empirical support on CelebA is suggestive but limited.

outputs: per-image DDIM noise and encoder embeddings for alignment analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Generative and encoding latent spaces share underlying Gaussian-like geometry with aligned linear directions
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; linear probes align, but broader applicability unclear

title: Agentic AI and the next intelligence explosion
task: Conceptual analysis of agentic AI as plural, social reasoning systems
key_results: No empirical results; position paper on institutional alignment and societies of thought
comments: Conceptual framing of AI singularity as plural societies rather than monolithic mind is novel; suggests institutional design as scaling path.

title: Effective Strategies for Asynchronous Software Engineering Agents
task: Coordinate asynchronous multi-agent collaboration for long-horizon software engineering via centralized delegation
key_results: 26.7% improvement on PaperBench, 14.3% on Commit0 over single-agent; branch-and-merge is critical coordination mechanism
comments: CAID framework elegantly applies git primitives (worktree, commit, merge) to multi-agent coordination; isolated workspaces prevent interference.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structured asynchronous coordination with isolated workspaces and test-based verification improves multi-agent SWE performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves significant accuracy gains

title: Scalable Prompt Routing via Fine-Grained Latent Task Discovery
task: Dynamic LLM selection for query routing across model pools
key_results: Outperforms baselines on 10 benchmarks with 11 models; achieves strongest model performance at <50% of its cost
comments: The two-stage architecture elegantly separates task discovery from quality estimation, though evaluation on frontier models with narrow gaps suggests diminishing returns in practice.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained latent task discovery enables better differentiation than manual taxonomies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via consistent outperformance across 10 benchmarks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Task-specific prediction heads improve routing accuracy over monolithic routers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables cost-performance tradeoff better than individual models

title: WorldCache: Content-Aware Caching for Accelerated Video World Models
task: Accelerate video world model inference via content-aware feature caching
key_results: 2.3x inference speedup on Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B while preserving 99.4% quality; outperforms prior training-free caching
comments: Motion-adaptive thresholds and saliency-weighted drift estimation address limitations of Zero-Order Hold assumption; phase-aware scheduling is principled.

model_io.model: WorldCache (motion-adaptive caching for DiT-based video models)
outputs: reused/blended/warped features for next denoising step

hypotheses.hypothesis: Motion-adaptive, saliency-weighted caching with blending/warping improves feature reuse quality in video diffusion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves major speedup with minimal quality loss

title: AnimalCLAP: Taxonomy-Aware Language-Audio Pretraining for Species Recognition and Trait Inference
task: Recognize animal species and infer ecological traits from vocalizations via taxonomy-aware language-audio pretraining
key_results: AnimalCLAP dataset of 4,225 hours (6,823 species) with 22 ecological traits; outperforms CLAP on species recognition
comments: Taxonomy-aware design leverages hierarchical biological structure; dataset and model will enable future ecosystem monitoring research.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Taxonomy-aware alignment improves zero-shot species recognition from unseen vocalizations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior generalization vs non-taxonomy CLAP

title: Perceptio: Perception Enhanced Vision Language Models via Spatial Token Generation
task: Enhance VLM spatial grounding via explicit semantic segmentation and depth token generation
key_results: +0.8/+1.4/+1.1 cIoU on RefCOCO/+/g; +10.3% HardBLINK; +1.0% MMBench
comments: Emitting spatial tokens before answering is intuitive chain-of-thought approach; composite depth-token objectives and soft-merging ensure stable training.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit spatial chain-of-thought via segmentation/depth tokens improves fine-grained spatial grounding in LVLMs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA across spatial reasoning benchmarks

title: MemDLM: Memory-Enhanced DLM Training
task: Train Diffusion Language Models with reduced train-inference mismatch via Parametric Memory from bi-level optimization
key_results: Faster convergence, lower training loss; emergent in-weight retrieval helps Needle-in-a-Haystack long-context tasks
comments: Embedding simulated denoising into training via bi-level optimization is principled; Parametric Memory acts as novel emergent mechanism.

model_io.model: MemDLM (Diffusion LM with bi-level memory optimization)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parametric Memory from bi-level optimization reduces train-inference mismatch in DLMs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables better convergence and long-context understanding

title: Demystifying Reinforcement Learning for Long-Horizon Tool-Using Agents: A Comprehensive Recipe
task: Systematically decompose agentic RL design space for long-horizon tool-using tasks
key_results: SOTA on TravelPlanner; reward/algorithm choices scale-dependent; ~1K balanced samples optimal; environmental stability critical
comments: Controlled empirical study provides practical recipe; key insights (scale-dependent reward shaping, sample efficiency) valuable for practitioners.

model_io.model: LLM agent with RL training on TravelPlanner environment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reward shaping, model scale, data composition, and algorithm choice interact in scale-dependent ways for agentic RL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; systematic study reveals optimal trade-offs

title: Not All Layers Are Created Equal: Adaptive LoRA Ranks for Personalized Image Generation
task: Adaptive LoRA ranks for personalized image generation via importance-ordered variational selection
key_results: Competitive visual quality across subjects with significantly lower memory and rank than fixed high-rank LoRA
comments: Encouraging adaptive ranks during fine-tuning avoids combinatorial search; importance ordering effectively prevents unnecessary rank growth.

model_io.model: LoRA² (diffusion model with adaptive per-layer ranks)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Allowing adaptive per-layer LoRA ranks during fine-tuning improves efficiency without quality loss
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves competitive quality with lower memory than fixed high-rank

title: SNAP: Speaker Nulling for Artifact Projection in Speech Deepfake Detection
task: Detect speech deepfakes via speaker subspace nulling to isolate synthesis artifacts
key_results: SOTA deepfake detection; reduces speaker entanglement to enable detection of unseen speakers
comments: Identifying speaker entanglement as key limitation is valuable; orthogonal projection cleanly separates speaker from synthesis artifact information.

model_io.model: SNAP (speaker subspace nulling via orthogonal projection)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Suppressing speaker-dependent components enables detectors to learn synthesis-artifact cues for better generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA on cross-speaker evaluation

title: Safe Flow Q-Learning: Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning with Reachability-Based Flow Policies
task: Offline safe RL via reachability-inspired safety value functions and one-step flow policies
key_results: Matches/exceeds prior safe RL performance with substantially lower inference latency; conformal calibration ensures safety coverage
comments: Combining Hamilton-Jacobi reachability with efficient one-step flow policies is elegant; important for real-time safety-critical deployment.

model_io.model: SafeFQL (safety value function + one-step flow policy)

hypotheses.hypothesis: One-step flow policies with reachability-based safety values enable efficient safe offline RL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves fast inference with safety guarantees

title: ToolRosetta: Bridging Open-Source Repositories and Large Language Model Agents through Automated Tool Standardization
task: Automatically standardize heterogeneous open-source code as MCP-compatible tools for LLM agents
key_results: Successfully automates tool standardization; improves task completion over commercial LLMs via tool integration
comments: Unified framework for diverse codebases via MCP; security inspection layer mitigates risks effectively.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Automated repository-to-tool translation enables reliable LLM agent access to diverse open-source code
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improves task completion over baseline LLMs

title: AdditiveLLM2: A Multi-modal Large Language Model for Additive Manufacturing
task: Domain-adapted LLM for additive manufacturing via instruction tuning on journal articles
key_results: >90% accuracy on additive manufacturing domain tasks; proficiency on language and vision tasks
comments: Accessible specialization method using 50M tokens from open-access data; demonstrates practical domain adaptation for technical domains.

model_io.model: AdditiveLLM2 (Gemma 3 instruction tuned on AM data)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Instruction tuning on domain-specific journal articles enables effective domain adaptation for technical fields
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves >90% on domain-specific benchmarks

title: FluidWorld: Reaction-Diffusion Dynamics as a Predictive Substrate for World Models
task: Video prediction via reaction-diffusion PDEs as alternative to Transformer-based predictors
key_results: 2x lower reconstruction error, 10-15% higher spatial structure preservation vs Transformer/ConvLSTM; maintains coherent multi-step rollouts
comments: PDE-based approach provides O(N) spatial complexity and adaptive computation vs O(N²) attention; interesting proof-of-concept for alternative architectures.

outputs: predicted future frames via PDE integration

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reaction-diffusion PDEs can match or exceed Transformer attention for video prediction with better multi-step stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms baselines on rollout coherence despite comparable parameters

title: Understanding Behavior Cloning with Action Quantization
task: Analyze quantization error propagation in behavior cloning with autoregressive models
key_results: Optimal sample complexity with log-loss; polynomial horizon dependence on quantization error under stability/smoothness conditions
comments: Theoretical analysis of widespread quantization practice fills important gap; proposes model-based augmentation without policy smoothness requirement.

model_io.model: Behavior cloning with action quantization (theoretical analysis)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Behavior cloning with quantized actions achieves optimal sample complexity under dynamical stability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; theoretical characterization matches empirical practice

title: Semantic Audio-Visual Navigation in Continuous Environments
task: Audio-visual navigation in continuous 3D environments with memory-augmented goal reasoning
key_results: 12.1% absolute improvement in success rate over SOTA; robust to short-duration sounds and long-distance navigation
comments: MAGNet design that jointly encodes spatial and semantic goals is well-motivated; memory-augmented reasoning handles silences effectively.

model_io.model: MAGNet (multimodal transformer with memory for audio-visual navigation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Memory-augmented goal reasoning improves audio-visual navigation robustness to sound intermittency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA with strong generalization

title: In-the-Wild Camouflage Attack on Vehicle Detectors through Controllable Image Editing
task: Generate adversarial camouflage for vehicles via conditional image editing with structural fidelity
key_results: >38% AP50 decrease on COCO/LINZ; better human-perceived stealthiness than baselines; transfers to black-box detectors
comments: Formulating camouflage as image-editing with unified objective (structure, style, adversarial) is effective; practical concerns about misuse.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Conditional image editing can generate semantically meaningful, transferable adversarial camouflage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves strong attack effectiveness with human stealthiness

title: Progressive Training for Explainable Citation-Grounded Dialogue: Reducing Hallucination to Zero in English-Hindi LLMs
task: Train explainable knowledge-grounded dialogue in bilingual setting with citation grounding and post-hoc analysis
key_results: Zero hallucination in encoder-decoder models from Stage 2; 0.0% hallucination rate with 14.60% WER for speech; prevents forgetting
comments: Progressive four-stage pipeline and systematic post-hoc explainability analysis (attention, Integrated Gradients, occlusion) provide transparency.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Citation-grounded SFT from Stage 2 onward eliminates hallucination in encoder-decoder models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 0.0% hallucination maintained through GRPO

title: Aperiodic Structures Never Collapse: Fibonacci Hierarchies for Lossless Compression
task: Lossless text compression using aperiodic hierarchical structures
key_results: Quasicryth achieves 22.59% on enwik9; aperiodic advantage grows to 11.1 MB savings at 1 GB scale versus period-5 baseline
comments: Theoretically elegant proof that Fibonacci tilings avoid collapse via Golden Compensation, but practical compression gains depend heavily on dataset size and whether deep hierarchy levels activate.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aperiodic Fibonacci hierarchies avoid finite-depth collapse of periodic tilings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; periodic tilings collapse after O(log p) levels, Fibonacci maintains positions at all scales

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dictionary reuse efficiency remains scale-invariant in Fibonacci hierarchies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via Golden Compensation property with asymptotic coverage Wφ/5

hypotheses.hypothesis: Deeper hierarchy levels activate more effectively in aperiodic structures at large scales
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 20.7 MB savings attributable to Fibonacci tiling on enwik9

title: Substrate-interacting pore loops of two ATPase subunits determine the degradation efficiency of the 26S proteasome
task: Characterize how pore-loop residues of individual ATPase subunits drive proteasome substrate capture and unfolding
key_results: Rpt6 and Rpt4 pore-1 loops play distinct roles in substrate capture versus unfolding; pore-loop contributions correlate with spiral-staircase ATPase positioning in substrate-free versus substrate-degrading states
comments: Integration of biochemical assays, single-molecule FRET, and cryo-EM provides multi-scale mechanistic insight, though causality between loop mutations and conformational dynamics remains partially inferred.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pore-1 loops of Rpt6 and Rpt4 are essential for substrate capture and mechanical unfolding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; distinct contributions to different degradation steps identified

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pore-loop function correlates with ATPase subunit position in spiral-staircase arrangement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; spatial positioning correlates with substrate-processing roles

title: Structure and function of mouse lens suture examined by 2-photon fluorescence microscopic imaging
task: Characterize lens suture architecture and fiber organization in wild-type versus KLPH-knockout mice using three-dimensional imaging
key_results: WT lenses show typical Y and double-Y anterior suture geometries; KLPH-KO lenses exhibit heterogeneous suture morphologies with markedly increased pattern randomization (SSIM p<0.05) and irregular central voids containing 2–5 μm aggregates
comments: 2PFM in vivo imaging reveals structural markers for suture cataracts, but lacks functional permeability measurements directly confirming the proposed microcirculation hypothesis.

hypotheses.hypothesis: KLPH protein is required for maintaining stable suture geometry and fiber organization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; KLPH-KO lenses show heterogeneous suture morphology and fiber misalignment

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lens sutures function as ion/nutrient transport pathways; loss of KLPH disrupts this function leading to cataract formation
hypotheses.result: Partially supported; morphological pathology evident but direct permeability measurements not conducted

title: Genetic diagnosis of sibling cases initiated by identification of outlier gene expression using transcriptome analysis of urine-derived cells
task: Genetic diagnosis of muscular dystrophy–dystroglycanopathy via urine RNA-seq outlier detection
key_results: OUTRIDER identified marked B3GALNT2 downregulation in proband; genome sequencing revealed 357-bp deletion in promoter; family segregation confirmed; RNA-seq supports aberrant splicing
comments: Proof-of-concept for UDCs RNA-seq-guided outlier analysis in non-coding variant discovery; demonstrates advantages of functional validation alongside genomics.

inputs: UDC transcriptomes from proband, affected brother, parents
outputs: genes with outlier expression, B3GALNT2 downregulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNA-seq outlier analysis can identify non-coding second hits in unsolved genetic disorders
hypotheses.result: OUTRIDER detected B3GALNT2 downregulation; genome sequencing confirmed segregating promoter deletion

title: Post-pandemic changes in population immunity have reduced the likelihood of emergence of zoonotic coronaviruses
task: Impact of post-pandemic population cross-immunity on zoonotic sarbecovirus emergence likelihood
key_results: Cross-neutralizing antibodies against zoonotic sarbecovirus spike proteins detected in COVID-19 sera; cross-immunity significantly reduces SARS-CoV-X emergence probability
comments: Mathematical modeling with empirical serology data shows vaccination may suppress emergence through cross-protection but high-specificity vaccines could paradoxically increase risk.

outputs: sarbecovirus emergence probability, impact of vaccine specificity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Population cross-immunity from COVID-19 reduces zoonotic sarbecovirus emergence
hypotheses.result: Cross-neutralizing antibodies present; modeling predicts emergence probability reduced by cross-immunity threshold

title: Ribonuclease 4 Functions in Nociceptor-Mediated Nerve Homeostasis
task: Functional characterization of ribonuclease 4 in nociceptor biology and myelin integrity
key_results: RNase4 selectively expressed in unmyelinated nociceptors; RNase4 deletion alters mechanical sensation in neuropathic pain model; non-cell-autonomous effects on myelin
comments: Single-cell transcriptomics and knockout analysis reveal dual roles in nociceptor function and nerve homeostasis; provides molecular target for pain regulation.

inputs: RNase4-deficient and wild-type nociceptor and myelin samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNase4 regulates nociceptor identity and function in a cell-autonomous manner
hypotheses.result: RNase4-deficient mice show altered nociceptor function and mechanical sensation

title: AI-based chest X-ray prioritization in the lung cancer diagnostic pathway: the LungIMPACT randomized controlled trial
task: AI-based CXR prioritization for lung cancer diagnostic pathway optimization
key_results: AI prioritization did not significantly reduce time to CT (ratio 0.97, P=0.31) or diagnosis (ratio 0.98, P=0.84); discordance AI–radiology 30.3%, actionable findings 23.9%
comments: Large RCT (93,326 CXRs) shows worklist prioritization alone insufficient; human-in-the-loop review critical; findings inform realistic AI deployment expectations in primary care.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI prioritization of CXR findings accelerates time to CT and lung cancer diagnosis
hypotheses.result: No significant difference in median times (53 vs 53 days CT, 44 vs 46 days diagnosis)

title: Clustering the protein universe of life using DIAMOND DeepClust
task: Planetary-scale protein clustering and organization
key_results: 544 million nonsingleton clusters from 19 billion biosphere proteins; clustering enhances AlphaFold2 structure prediction
comments: Ultra-fast cascaded method scales to trillions; database availability supports reproducible structural prediction workflows.

inputs: clustered protein sequences from DeepClust database

title: 2D Multimodal Image Collection for Fluorescence Prediction from Transmitted Light Microscopy
task: Fluorescence prediction from transmitted light microscopy
key_results: 56,984 standardized 2D images across 2,574 acquisition sets from 30 studies; multimodal pairs enable deep learning for in silico labeling
comments: Large-scale, well-annotated dataset addresses reproducibility in microscopy ML; real-world variability enhances generalizability.

inputs: transmitted light images (bright-field, phase contrast, DIC)

title: Advanced analysis and detection of cervical cancer using NASNetLarge
task: Deep learning-based cervical cancer detection from histology images
key_results: NASNetLarge transfer learning achieves 94% training accuracy, 98.33% validation accuracy with data augmentation
comments: Transfer learning from ImageNet-pretrained NASNetLarge effectively generalizes to cervical cancer classification; dataset-specific top-layer tuning critical for task.

title: Design, synthesis, molecular docking and cytotoxic evaluation of novel pyrimidine-based sulfonamide derivatives as potent anticancer agents: SAR insights and biological profiling
task: Design and evaluation of novel anticancer pyrimidine-sulfonamide derivatives
key_results: Compounds 18, 21, 23, 24 show IC50 7.4–10.2 µM vs HepG2, comparable to 5-FU; compound 23 docking score −5.56 kcal/mol against thymidylate synthase
comments: Structure–activity relationship analysis identifies electron-donating groups as key; molecular docking validates binding mechanism but requires in vivo confirmation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Electron-donating groups and balanced lipophilicity enhance cytotoxicity and selectivity
hypotheses.result: SAR analysis confirms electron-donating groups correlate with increased activity

title: A length-biased Sujit distribution framework: properties, simulation-based inference, and application to clinical remission data
task: Length-biased probability distribution framework for clinical remission time-to-event analysis
key_results: Length-Biased Sujit distribution (LBSJT) provides superior fit to clinical remission data vs. conventional models; Monte Carlo simulation confirms estimation robustness
comments: Generalized weighted probability distribution enhances flexibility for survival data; theoretical properties (moments, reliability measures, curves) fully developed.

model_io.model: Length-Biased Sujit (LBSJT) distribution + maximum likelihood estimation
outputs: parameter estimates, goodness-of-fit metrics, reliability curves

title: ForamJ – A tool for the reproducible, semi-automated analysis of foraminifera micro computed tomography datasets
task: Semi-automated reproducible analysis of foraminiferal micro-CT datasets
key_results: ForamJ achieves 0.9981 segmentation accuracy, 0.97 Dice score, 1.30% difference from manual; 154 s median processing per sample
comments: Open-source ImageJ plugin streamlines high-throughput µCT analysis with established micropaleontological metrics; batch processing enables scalable fossil analysis.

model_io.model: ForamJ (ImageJ plugin): segmentation, chamber isolation, morphological quantification
outputs: calcite volume, chamber volumes, surface area, wall thickness, chamber centroids

title: Automated real-time surveillance of Bithynia snails using a comparative YOLO based approach for liver fluke host detection
task: Automated detection and classification of Bithynia snails for liver fluke surveillance
key_results: YOLOv10 achieves 98.7% classification accuracy, 31.9 MB model, 4.54 FPS; humans perfect detection (100%), AI superior classification (74.5% vs 48.3%)
comments: Hybrid human–AI workflow (human detection + AI classification) achieves higher accuracy than either alone; establishes foundation for scalable O. viverrini surveillance.

inputs: 4,204 images with 8,559 annotated Bithynia specimens (three species)
outputs: species classification (B. funiculata, B. siamensis goniomphalos, B. s. siamensis)

title: Lightweight and interpretable edge intelligence AI with intrusion detection for trustworthy cardiac arrhythmia in medical IoT
task: Lightweight interpretable arrhythmia detection for medical IoT with adversarial robustness
key_results: CLARITY-AI 2.0 achieves F1 0.928, AUC 0.985 (MIT-BIH); generalizes zero-shot (AUC 0.940 PTB-XL, 0.915 Chapman); 350 KB footprint, 8.1 ms latency on ESP32
comments: Intrusion detection module flags unreliable inputs under cyber-attack; SHAP+LLM explanations enhance clinician interpretability; real-time edge feasibility demonstrated.

title: Wearable sensor big data analysis reveals spatiotemporal injury patterns in professional tennis players
task: Spatiotemporal injury pattern analysis in professional tennis using wearable sensor big data
key_results: Seasonal periodicity in injury; 47.3% shoulder-elbow, 31.8% lumbar-hip concentration; 73.2% injury variance explained by spatiotemporal coupling; Transformer model 91.5% accuracy, AUC 0.956
comments: LSTM and ensemble methods identify complex injury patterns; Transformer-based prediction significantly outperforms traditional statistical methods; framework enables intelligent prevention systems.

model_io.model: LSTM networks, clustering, ensemble methods; Transformer-based prediction
inputs: wearable sensor measurements (biomechanics, physiology, movement) from training and competition
outputs: injury pattern maps (temporal, spatial, coupled); injury risk predictions

title: Mathematical modeling of sequential Dengue–Zika infections: dynamic insights into antibody-dependent enhancement and neutralization effects
task: Mathematical modeling of sequential dengue–zika infections and antibody-dependent effects
key_results: Model calibrated to Brazil data (Jan–Sep 2021); global sensitivity analysis identifies ADE threshold: above which antibody-dependent neutralization dominates
comments: Sequential infection framework captures both enhancement and neutralization; PRCC and LHS sensitivity analysis clarifies disease drivers for vaccine development.

outputs: disease equilibrium points, infection dynamics forecasts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Antibody-dependent enhancement has obstructive effect on sequential infections
hypotheses.result: ADE can contribute to neutralization above a threshold (ADN effect), modifying transmission

title: A species rules syntax model accurately organizes birdsong syllables into songs
task: Prediction of birdsong syntax from syllable acoustics and sequence position
key_results: Species rules model predicts syllable sequence as accurately as tutor songs in two estrildid finch species; high consistency across colonies
comments: Natural language processing approach demonstrates species-specific syntax rules are learnable from acoustic–positional correlations alone.

inputs: sequence position correlations across conspecific individuals
outputs: predicted syllable sequences in adult songs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Species-specific rules based on syllable acoustics and sequence position explain song syntax specification
hypotheses.result: Model predictions match tutor and adult songs equally well; rules generalize across colonies

title: Novel explainable deep learning based drug sensitivity prediction for early treatment of breast cancer
task: Explainable deep learning drug sensitivity prediction for breast cancer
key_results: EDrGAT model achieves R² 0.93 for IC50 prediction, outperforming state-of-the-art; catboost regressor performs best for imputation
comments: Graph attention transformer with task-specific feature engineering handles missing data better than conventional DL; SHAP explanations enhance clinical interpretability.

inputs: temporal pharmacodynamics features (lag, rolling mean, exponential mean)
imputation candidates for missing drug-sensitivity data

title: In silico evaluation of FDA-approved antivirals and corticosteroids against SARS-CoV-2
task: Computational evaluation of FDA-approved antivirals and corticosteroids against SARS-CoV-2
key_results: Baloxavir Marboxil highest docking affinity (−8.88 kcal/mol); dexamethasone most stable in MD (RMSD 0.78 Å); all three show stable binding (RMSD <3 Å)
comments: Integrated docking, MD, DFT and ADMET analyses provide convergent evidence; results support experimental validation of BM and DM as candidates.

outputs: RMSD, binding stability, root mean square deviation

title: Uncovering potentially targetable genes in liver fibrosis via bioinformatics and experimental validation
task: Identification of targetable genes in liver fibrosis via multi-dataset integration and validation
key_results: Eight genes (AQP1, CCL19, CXCL6, CXCL9, CXCL10, EPCAM, IGJ, LUM) upregulated with good diagnostic ROC performance; enriched in immune regulation, ECM, inflammation
comments: Batch-corrected multi-dataset integration with WGCNA and limma identifies clinically validated targets; IHC/IF confirms AQP1 upregulation in tissues.

model_io.model: Batch correction, limma (differential expression), WGCNA (co-expression modules)
outputs: DEGs, co-expression modules; intersection with druggable genes

title: Machine learning approach for adult age estimation using dental characteristics on panoramic radiographs
task: Machine learning models for adult age estimation from dental panoramic radiographs
key_results: Gradient boosting achieves RMSE 10.88 years, R² 0.70 vs linear regression RMSE 12.04, R² 0.63 (n=2,415); sound/missing teeth most influential (SHAP)
comments: Dual-feature one-hot encoding preserves dental information better than conventional approaches; SHAP analysis clarifies key predictors; models should be adjunctive tools only.

model_io.model: Linear regression and six ML models (random forest, XGBoost, KNN, SVM, decision tree, MLP)
inputs: panoramic radiographs, encoded dental characteristics (n=2,415, age 20–89)

title: Stability and robustness of minimal majority vote interpretable ensembles
task: Stability and robustness assessment of minimal majority-vote interpretable ensembles
key_results: Minimal ensembles exhibit low bootstrap stability and high multiplicity in low-sample regimes; robustness degrades with feature noise
comments: Study highlights that minimal solutions prioritize accuracy over stability; explainability in high-stakes settings requires explicit stability reporting.

inputs: synthetic benchmarks (d=8–10, n=10–500) and UCI datasets
outputs: classification predictions; multiplicity rate, bootstrap stability (Jaccard), feature-flip robustness metrics

hypotheses.hypothesis: Minimal ensemble solutions are stable and robust across perturbations
hypotheses.result: Minimal solutions fit data accurately but show low bootstrap stability and high sensitivity to noise

title: Temporal Learning with Dynamic Range (TLDR) for modeling recurrent exposure and treatment outcomes
task: Time-sensitive machine learning for predicting post-acute sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 (PASC)
key_results: TLDR framework achieves AUROC 0.791 vs 0.668 benchmark (18.4% improvement); PRAUC 0.590 vs 0.421 (40.14% gain); improved generalizability on 85,000+ patients
comments: Temporal feature engineering captures exposure–outcome dynamics better than atemporal ML; framework generalizes across EHR systems and is available in MLHO R package.

inputs: longitudinal EHR data (time-stamped clinical events, exposures, treatments)
outputs: PASC risk predictions, time-stamped feature importances

hypotheses.hypothesis: Incorporating temporal sequence improves PASC risk prediction vs. atemporal approaches
hypotheses.result: TLDR achieves 18.4% AUROC and 40.14% PRAUC improvements over benchmark

title: Optimising pandemic response through vaccination strategies using neural networks
task: Neural network optimization of pandemic vaccination strategies under health-economic constraints
key_results: Physics-Informed Neural Network optimal strategy reduces hospital-days 85.3%, deaths 84.4%, total costs 22.3% vs no-vaccination (4–6 pp above government rollout)
comments: Deep neural network directly approximates time-varying vaccination rates; PINN parameter calibration embeds epidemiological dynamics efficiently; framework generalizable to future pandemics.

inputs: stochastic SVEI3RD model equations (8 state variables), COVID-19 Victoria data

outputs: time-varying vaccination rates minimizing total pandemic expenditure

title: A machine learning-derived biological age model for liver grafts provides a superior assessment of aging compared to chronological age in transplantation
task: Machine learning prediction of liver graft biological age and clinical outcomes
key_results: Gradient boosting model biological age predicts biliary complications (AUC 0.80) and graft loss; accelerated aging (BA>CA) is independent risk factor (HR 4.188)
comments: Nonlinear ML models capture aging heterogeneity missed by chronological age; biological age demonstrates superior clinical utility than standard metrics.

model_io.model: Multiple linear regression and four genetic algorithm-optimized ML models (gradient boosting best)
outputs: biological age (BA); prediction of biliary complications, graft survival

hypotheses.hypothesis: Biological age derived from nonlinear models better predicts graft outcomes than chronological age
hypotheses.result: GB model biological age predicts complications and survival; CA does not

title: Clinical utility of foundation models in musculoskeletal MRI for biomarker fidelity and predictive outcomes
task: Foundation model-based automated musculoskeletal MRI biomarker measurement and clinical decision support
key_results: Fine-tuned segmenters (SAM, MedSAM) achieve clinically reliable measurements; 48-month landmark models forecast knee replacement with favorable calibration; three-stage triage reduces workload
comments: Model-agnostic framework converts routine MRI into standardized biomarkers; demonstrates path from automated measurement to patient risk stratification.

model_io.model: Fine-tuned foundation segmenters (SAM, SAM2, MedSAM) + automated detection
outputs: cartilage, bone, soft tissue biomarkers; segmentation masks
knee replacement risk, incident osteoarthritis probability

title: Multi-omics evaluation of cell lines as models for metastatic prostate cancer
task: Systematic evaluation of prostate cancer cell lines and models for metastatic disease
key_results: PC3 cell line shows poor transcriptomic/epigenomic similarity to metastatic subtypes; selected organoids better recapitulate mesenchymal stem-like profiles than engineered cell lines
comments: Large-scale multi-omics comparison reveals PC3 limitations and identifies hypermutation influence on tumor microenvironment; organoids preferred for mesenchymal subtype modeling.

inputs: genomics, transcriptomics, epigenomics from cell lines and metastatic patient samples
outputs: similarity metrics (genomic, transcriptomic, epigenomic), subtype classification

hypotheses.hypothesis: Widely used cell lines faithfully represent metastatic prostate cancer subtypes
hypotheses.result: PC3 exhibits poor similarity; engineered stem-like lines fail to recapitulate mesenchymal profiles; selected organoids show higher fidelity

title: BODIES: BOdy shape parameter and 3D meshes of Individuals basEd on SUPR
task: Synthetic 3D avatar dataset based on SUPR body model
key_results: 84,000 synthetic subjects with SUPR parameters; framework shows improvements over state-of-the-art for 3D avatar generation
comments: Large synthetic dataset addresses SUPR adoption gap, but synthetic-to-real transfer and quantitative improvement metrics are not detailed.

title: A Topology Standardized 3D Facial Dataset with Emotion and Action Unit Diversity for East Asians
task: Standardized 3D facial dataset with topology consistency for East Asians
key_results: 98 subjects with 6 emotions + 9 FACS action units; unified topology via FLAME + Edge-NICP enables point-to-point comparisons
comments: High-quality topology-standardized 3D face data is valuable for graphics and HCI, though the modest sample size limits generalization across ethnic diversity.

title: Enhanced content-based image retrieval via hybrid color, texture, and deep learning features
task: Content-based image retrieval via hybrid handcrafted and deep features
key_results: CTD-Net: Corel-1K 98.85%, Corel-10K 92.40%, Caltech-101 88.94% precision; outperforms existing methods via color, texture, and EfficientNet-B7 fusion
comments: Feature fusion (Color Histogram + Moments + LBP + Wavelet + EfficientNet-B7) is well-motivated; high precision across datasets is encouraging, though computational cost vs. real-time CBIR trade-off is not discussed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hybrid handcrafted + deep feature fusion improves CBIR precision
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 98.85% on Corel-1K, outperforming prior methods

title: Computational investigation on the hydrodynamic performance of a vertically submerged plate-type wave energy converter under variable relative openings
task: Wave energy converter hydrodynamic efficiency with variable relative openings
key_results: Optimal efficiency at relative opening α₂=50%; axial flow velocity peaks at T=1.87s for increased wave steepness; improved efficiency with higher wave steepness
comments: VOF-based numerical wave tank is rigorous; findings are specific to geometry and wave conditions tested; cost-benefit of WEC design optimization trade-offs is not discussed.

model_io.model: ANSYS Fluent 2024 R2 (VOF interface tracking + Stokes 2nd-order waves)
inputs: wave period, height, relative opening, seabed profile

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimal relative opening exists for maximum energy extraction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed at α₂=50%

title: Neuroimaging-driven recommendation systems for personalized sports training and injury prevention
task: Neuroimaging-driven personalized sports training recommendation system
key_results: NeuroAthleteNet + NeuroSportSync multimodal framework improves predictive accuracy and interpretability; outperforms traditional neuroimaging methods
comments: Spatiotemporal neural features + graph connectivity analysis is conceptually sound; practical applicability to real-world athletes and concussion risk modeling requires validation.

model_io.model: NeuroAthleteNet (spatiotemporal features + graph connectivity) + NeuroSportSync (multimodal sync)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Neuroimaging-biomechanical integration improves training personalization
hypotheses.result: Improved predictive accuracy and interpretability vs. neuroimaging-only methods

title: Adaptive training load optimization for track and field athletes: A reinforcement learning approach
task: Reinforcement learning-based adaptive training load optimization for track/field athletes
key_results: DQN-based offline policy finder reduces training error; effective injury risk management; maintains performance within optimal range dynamically
comments: Digital twin + dual reward function (performance + health) is well-designed; 25 athletes over 1 season is modest for RL validation; offline-to-online deployment risk is not addressed.

model_io.model: DQN (Q-value feedforward network + ε-Greedy + Experience Replay)
inputs: HRV, sleep, training loads, ACWR, performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual reward function balances short-term performance and long-term health
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; loss converged to near-zero; injury risk reduced with maintained performance

title: A blockchain-integrated multi-scale convolutional network for accurate recognition and secure tracking of high-difficulty movements in aerobics training
task: Blockchain-integrated CNN for aerobics movement recognition and data security
key_results: 96.8% recognition accuracy on custom aerobics dataset; blockchain integration increases traceability efficiency by 38.2%
comments: Fusion of spatiotemporal CNN with blockchain for movement analysis is interesting, but traceability efficiency metric is not clearly defined and blockchain overhead is not quantified.

model_io.model: Multi-channel multi-scale CNN + dual attention + blockchain

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual attention mechanism enhances complex movement recognition
hypotheses.result: 96.8% accuracy achieved, outperforming traditional methods

title: Analytical modeling of pcm-based cooling system for lithium-ion batteries
task: Analytical modeling of PCM-based cooling for lithium-ion batteries
key_results: Perturbation + Green's function methods predict temperature profiles and phase-change front position; <5% error vs. experiments; core remains hotter than surface
comments: Analytical solution avoids numerical computation; practical insight that battery core heating limits PCM effectiveness is valuable, though material parameter sensitivity is not fully explored.

model_io.model: Perturbation + Green's function coupled heat transfer solver

hypotheses.hypothesis: PCM surface cooling is decoupled from core heating
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improving battery thermal conductivity is crucial

title: Reading the climate room through unsupervised analysis of unfiltered climate perspectives
task: Unsupervised analysis of climate discourse perspectives
key_results: Semantic chunking algorithm segments 48,443 documents; advocates emphasize solutions/crisis framing, skeptics deploy anti-elite rhetoric
comments: Large-scale dataset and novel chunking method advance climate NLP, though rhetorical patterns may reflect curated advocacy/skeptic sources rather than true population discourse.

title: Real-time underwater object detection via frequency-domain dynamics and spatially enhanced feature modulation
task: Real-time underwater object detection with frequency-domain dynamics
key_results: mAP 72.1% on UTDAC2020 (1.7% above baseline); 27.1% parameter reduction, 24.6% FLOP reduction, 72.6 FPS inference
comments: Lightweight and efficient design balances accuracy with latency; frequency-domain modulation is well-motivated for noisy underwater imagery.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Frequency-domain convolution reduces high-frequency noise while preserving edges
hypotheses.result: mAP improved by 1.7% with 24.6% FLOP reduction

title: A bearing fault diagnosis method for complex system based on improved extended belief rule base
task: Bearing fault diagnosis with improved belief rule base and ensemble undersampling
key_results: KL-divergence EBRB-EU outperforms Euclidean-distance EBRB; effective class imbalance mitigation; strong noise resistance
comments: KL divergence for probability distribution matching is theoretically sound; ensemble undersampling + differential evolution is pragmatic, though dataset imbalance levels vary.

hypotheses.hypothesis: KL divergence better captures signal distribution differences than Euclidean distance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved accuracy on imbalanced datasets

title: A unified time series classification framework via adaptive Gaussian image representation
task: Unified time series classification via adaptive Gaussian image representation
key_results: TS2Vision achieves Rank-1 consistency on 158 UCR/UEA datasets; Lipschitz continuity proven; outperforms state-of-the-art baselines
comments: Novel Gaussian + circle-packing approach elegantly bridges time-series and vision; theoretical stability guarantee is valuable, though computational cost vs. baselines is not quantified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive Gaussian mapping satisfies Lipschitz continuity
hypotheses.result: Proven; ensures representation stability

title: A SMT pin soldering defect detection system based on improved connectivity domain algorithm
task: SMT pin soldering defect detection via connected domain + watershed segmentation
key_results: Improved algorithm reduces false-positive rate in dense soldering; maintains traditional accuracy; stronger adaptability and robustness to noise interference
comments: Watershed + connected domain hybrid addresses dense assembly misjudgment; LabVIEW implementation is pragmatic, but quantitative false-positive/true-positive trade-off metrics are not reported.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Watershed segmentation mitigates connected-domain misjudgment in dense soldering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced false-positive rate with maintained accuracy

title: A scalable hybrid computational intelligence framework with bio inspired optimization for high dimensional malicious URL inference
task: Malicious URL detection using hybrid discriminant analysis and bio-inspired optimization
key_results: Bio-inspired optimized model: accuracy 96.35%, precision 96.54%, recall 96.35%, F1 0.964, specificity 96.36% on 63,191 URLs
comments: LDA/QDA + categorical boosting + Mother/Osprey algorithms with SHAP attribution is comprehensive; statistical rigor via Shapiro-Wilk and Kruskal-Wallis strengthens robustness claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Bio-inspired optimization improves hybrid discriminant inference for URL classification
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 96.35% accuracy with statistically validated feature importance

title: Water treatment chemical supply chain disruption risk assessment and mitigation strategy ranking using BWM–VIKOR
task: Water treatment chemical supply chain disruption mitigation ranking
key_results: BWM-VIKOR framework ranks mitigation strategies; compliance/health (0.26) and service continuity (0.18) are top priorities; dual sourcing achieves Q=0.083
comments: Multi-criteria decision-making approach provides transparent utility planning support, though case study is single-site and sensitivity analysis could be more comprehensive.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Compliance/public health is the dominant decision criterion
hypotheses.result: Weight 0.26 confirmed via BWM

title: Collective and augmented intelligence outperform artificial intelligence on emotion recognition tests
task: Collective and augmented intelligence in emotion recognition vs. AI
key_results: GPT-5 mini outperforms individual humans on RMET/MRMET; human plurality voting significantly outperforms GPT-5 mini; augmented human+AI aggregation outperforms either alone
comments: Wisdom-of-crowds finding challenges evaluation methodologies; aggregation paradox is striking but may reflect task-specific properties and sampling noise.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Human collective intelligence exceeds individual AI on forced-choice emotion tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via plurality voting; AI advantage reversed at aggregate level

title: Role of FePt grain size on writing performance for next-generation magnetic recording technology
task: FePt grain size optimization for heat-assisted magnetic recording
key_results: 5 nm grains achieve areal density 16.4 Tb/in²; 3–4 nm grains offer higher density but higher BER; higher write field/time mitigates BER
comments: Atomistic modeling with VAMPIRE software provides fine-grained optimization guidance; master equation calibration validates simulation but practical manufacturability is not discussed.

model_io.model: VAMPIRE atomistic spin simulator + master equation

hypotheses.hypothesis: 5 nm grains minimize BER while maintaining high areal density
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; smaller grains trade BER for density gains

title: A novel nowcasting (estimation) model based on an adaptive network neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy inference system (ANNHFIS): a case study of Istanbul
task: NO₂ nowcasting near biomass plants via neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy neuro-fuzzy inference
key_results: ANNHFIS-PSO: RMSE 3.6488 µg/m³, R² 0.8938; lowest RMSE vs. MLP-ANN, ANFIS variants, LSTM; neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy sets capture environmental uncertainty
comments: Novel ANFIS variant incorporating neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy membership functions is theoretically interesting; PSO + Adam hybrid learning is pragmatic, but model interpretability vs. black-box forecasting trade-off is not explored.

model_io.model: ANNHFIS-PSO (neuro-fuzzy + neutrosophic hesitant membership + PSO + Adam)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Neutrosophic hesitant fuzzy sets improve air quality nowcasting under uncertainty
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; RMSE 3.6488 µg/m³, R² 0.8938, outperforming baselines

title: A brain–edge co-evolution framework for zero-trust real-time hot patching in power equipment
task: Zero-trust hot patching for power equipment with brain-edge co-evolution
key_results: 12.3 ms decision latency (<<1s real-time requirement); 3.5% entropy increase risk; 99.99% service availability on 1,200 operations
comments: Novel brain-EEG + negative entropy + eBPF framework bridges human intuition and real-time constraints; safety-critical validation on RTDS/IED is reassuring, but generalization to other grid topologies is unclear.

model_io.model: Brain-edge co-evolution framework (EEG + eBPF atomic patching + entropy model)
outputs: hot patch decision + structural order metric

hypotheses.hypothesis: Human EEG-derived risk intuition accelerates zero-trust decisions for real-time power systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 12.3 ms latency << 1s control deadline; 99.99% availability maintained

title: Euler simulation of hydrocyclone pre-separation before oily wastewater membrane treatment based on Population balance model
task: Hydrocyclone pre-separation for oily wastewater before membrane treatment
key_results: Oil phase distribution controlled by inlet velocity, oil content, cone angle, overflow depth; 5–15 m/s optimal for coalescence; >20 m/s causes shear breakup and overflow switching
comments: Euler simulation + population balance model captures complex multiphase flow; practical insight that coalescence vs. shear trade-off limits separation, but membrane fouling mitigation efficiency is not quantified.

model_io.model: Finite volume method (Euler) + population balance model
inputs: inlet flow velocity, oil content, geometry
outputs: oil phase trajectory, volume fraction distribution, separation efficiency

hypotheses.hypothesis: Oil-content-dependent coalescence/shear balance optimizes hydrocyclone pre-separation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; low-medium velocities enhance coalescence; high velocities cause breakup

title: A Monolithic CMOS-MEMS SoC with 1.8 mm/s and 2 mK Resolution for Flow and Temperature Sensing via a Microcantilever Array
task: Develop monolithic CMOS-MEMS SoC for multiparameter flow and temperature sensing
key_results: Temperature sensitivity 25.1 kHz/°C (resolution 2.3 mK); flow sensitivity 32.76 kHz/(m/s) at 130 m/s (minimum detectable velocity 1.8 mm/s); integrated cantilever array with on-chip signal processing in 0.18 µm CMOS
comments: Impressive monolithic integration demonstrates practical CMOS-MEMS for high-precision sensing, though limited to low-frequency measurements and single-beam cantilever readout.

model_io.model: CMOS-MEMS SoC with capacitive microcantilever array

hypotheses.hypothesis: Monolithic CMOS-MEMS enables ultrahigh sensitivity without external electronics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 2.3 mK temperature resolution and 1.8 mm/s flow resolution achieved

title: Sperm hyperactivation drives a circling-and-wandering swimming behavior
task: Characterize sperm swimming gaits and their migration efficiency
key_results: Three gaits identified: wandering (10× higher diffusivity than circling), circling, and circling-and-wandering; circling-and-wandering mode optimizes spreading in porous media with increasing geometric complexity
comments: Elegant demonstration that stochastic motility switching provides adaptive advantage in complex environments, though biological triggers for transitions remain unclear.

model_io.model: Bull sperm in microfluidic chambers and porous media simulations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hyperactivation drives circling-and-wandering behavior
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; three distinct gaits observed with hyperactivation-dependent transitions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined gait provides better migration in complex environments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by stochastic transport simulations

title: Tunable and nonlinearity-enhanced dispersive-plus-dissipative coupling in photon-pressure circuits
task: Tunable photon-pressure coupling in superconducting circuits
key_results: Magnetic-flux-tunable dispersive-dissipative coupling with multi-photon enhancement scaling stronger than √nc; Fano-like photon-pressure induced transparency and parametric instability observed
comments: Demonstrates novel nonlinearity-enhanced interaction paths not accessible in standard optomechanical systems, though practical applications remain to be established.

model_io.model: Photon-pressure circuit (GHz coupled to MHz circuit)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonlinearities enhance multi-photon coupling beyond √nc scaling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by experimental coupling rate enhancement

title: Emergent discrete space-time crystal of Majorana-like quasiparticles in chiral liquid crystals
task: Observe discrete space-time crystals in driven chiral liquid crystals
key_results: Classical analogs of 1+1D and 2+1D discrete space-time crystals with topological Majorana-like quasiparticle features; period-doubling from periodic soliton-disclination transformations; robustness against temporal perturbations
comments: Remarkable demonstration that spontaneous space-time symmetry breaking extends to classical soft matter via Floquet driving, opening new avenues for time crystal studies.

model_io.model: Liquid crystal system under oscillatory Floquet driving

hypotheses.hypothesis: Period-doubling arises from Majorana-like topological features
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by observation of periodic soliton-disclination transformations

title: Modulation of quantum geometry and its coupling to pseudo-electric field by dynamic strain
task: Demonstrate real-time modulation of quantum geometry and pseudo-electric field coupling
key_results: Berry curvature and dipole dynamically modulated by oscillatory strain; pseudo-electric field generated and coupled to quantum geometry; Hall signals at linear combinations of strain/field frequencies
comments: Opens powerful control pathway for quantum geometric properties beyond static perturbations, though microscopic mechanism of strain-induced pseudo-fields requires clarification.

model_io.model: 2D heterostructure on oscillating membrane with in-plane AC field

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic strain modulates Berry curvature in real-time
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by modulated Hall signals at strain frequency combinations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pseudo-electric field couples to Berry curvature
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by anomalous dynamic Hall response without applied field

title: Linking photoelectron circular dichroism to the asymmetric total photoemission yield measured in aerosol nanoparticles of tyrosine
task: Link photoelectron circular dichroism to chiral asymmetry of photoemission yield in aerosol nanoparticles
key_results: Chiral asymmetry in photoelectron angular distribution (PECD) translates to measurable chiral asymmetry of total photoemission yield (CAPY); vacuum-free detection of molecular chirality via aerosol measurements
comments: Innovative approach enabling chirality analysis without electron spectrometers or vacuum, though quantitative enantiopurity determination requires further development.

model_io.model: Tyrosine aerosol nanoparticles under circular polarized light

hypotheses.hypothesis: PECD-induced angular asymmetry manifests in total photoemission yield
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CAPY detectable in aerosol particles

title: Crystal symmetry-dependent Orbital Rashba Edelstein effect in epitaxial CuO thin film
task: Reveal crystal symmetry effects on orbital Rashba-Edelstein response
key_results: Four-fold anisotropic sign change in spin torque efficiency in epitaxial CuO; areal density of 16.4 Tb/in² with optimized 5 nm grains
comments: First demonstration of crystalline symmetry explicitly governing orbital angular momentum anisotropy, though microscopic mechanism of symmetry breaking requires deeper theoretical understanding.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Crystal symmetry determines orbital Rashba-Edelstein anisotropy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; sign reversal and four-fold anisotropy observed experimentally and computationally

title: Phonon thermal Hall effect: the roles of disorder, annealing, and metallic contacts
task: Identify disorder and strain as suppressors of phonon thermal Hall effect
key_results: High-κ SrTiO₃ exhibits thermal Hall angle up to 0.3% at 9 T; disordered samples show negligible effect; annealing restores ~0.1% signal with minimal change in κ; metallic/insulating contacts yield identical results
comments: Definitively rules out parasitic contact effects and reveals extreme sensitivity to structural disorder, constraining theoretical models of phonon Berry curvature.

model_io.model: SrTiO₃ crystal with variable disorder and metallic/insulating contacts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thermal Hall effect is intrinsic and decouples from longitudinal κ
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by annealing restoring THE while leaving κ unchanged

hypotheses.hypothesis: Contact effects are negligible for THE measurement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by identical results with metallic and insulating contacts

title: Symmetry-broken Kondo screening and zero-energy mode in a kagome superconductor
task: Probe Kondo screening symmetry breaking and zero-energy modes in kagome superconductor
key_results: Spatial structure of Kondo screening breaks all in-plane mirror symmetries near Cr impurities; zero-bias conductance peak from V vacancies; evidence for electronic chirality from orbital loop currents
comments: Elegant STM study linking local Kondo physics to global chirality, though direct connection between symmetry breaking and loop current order remains inferential.

model_io.model: CsV3Sb5 kagome superconductor with magnetic impurities (Cr) and vacancies (V)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magnetic impurities trigger Kondo screening that breaks mirror symmetry
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by STM imaging showing symmetry breaking in spatial Kondo pattern

hypotheses.hypothesis: Symmetry breaking originates from orbital loop current order
hypotheses.result: Supported by zero-energy modes and spatial structure analysis

title: Fluid–structure interaction and thermal performance: a numerical study on crossflow heat exchangers with aerodynamically optimised splitter elements
task: Optimize crossflow heat exchanger performance using aerodynamic splitter elements
key_results: Backwards-facing splitter plates with optimal L/D ratio enhance Nusselt number while maintaining PEC>1; longer plates yield superior PEC at moderate Reynolds numbers; Nu/f validation within 14%/12% of experiments
comments: Comprehensive 3D/2D CFD study reveals wake suppression and secondary vortex mechanisms, providing practical design guidelines though generalization to other geometries requires further work.

model_io.model: Crossflow heat exchanger with backwards-facing splitter plates (3D/2D turbulent RANS)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimal L/D balances heat transfer gains against pressure drop penalties
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PEC>1 across all configurations with maximum at intermediate L/D

title: Compact multi-band bandpass filter with independently controlled passbands using CRLH-TZRP
task: Design compact multi-band bandpass filter with independent passband control
key_results: CRLH-TZRP concept achieves independent tuning of three passbands for Wi-Fi (2.4 GHz), WiMAX (3.5 GHz), 5G (4.9 GHz); compact size 10.4 × 10.8 × 0.64 mm³; simulation/measurement agreement <5% deviation
comments: Elegant transmission-zero allocation strategy enables compact multi-band filtering, though third-order filter limits steepness compared to higher-order designs.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CRLH resonators enable independent passband control via transmission zero allocation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by measured responses matching design specifications

title: Chaotic fluctuations mark the sign of mental activity in task-based heart rate variability
task: Detect mental activity via nonlinear HRV analysis during cognitive tasks
key_results: Chaos/complexity indices increase significantly during mental tasks while conventional time/frequency-domain HRV remains unchanged; nonlinear measures uniquely sensitive to cognitive processes
comments: Demonstrates that chaos theory provides superior signal for brain-heart coupling absent in linear analyses, though mechanistic basis of chaotic HRV generation warrants investigation.

model_io.model: Heart rate variability (R-R intervals) during physical and mental tasks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonlinear measures are more sensitive to cognitive load than linear HRV
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; chaos indices show significant increase during mental tasks

title: Electronic, magnetic, optical, and thermoelectric properties of K₂OsCl₆ for spintronic and energy harvesting applications
task: Predict spintronic and energy-harvesting properties of double perovskite K₂OsCl₆
key_results: Ferromagnetic ground state with ~1.99 µB/f.u.; half-metallic with 100% spin polarization; Seebeck ~180 µV/K at 900 K; low thermal conductivity 2.8 W m⁻¹ K⁻¹
comments: DFT predicts promising spintronic material but experimental validation and stability analysis against oxidation required.

hypotheses.hypothesis: K₂OsCl₆ exhibits half-metallicity with Os-dominated magnetism
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by spin-resolved band structure showing ~100% spin polarization

title: Radiation-resistant C-type dipole dump magnet for MIR/THz FEL applications at TARLA
task: Design radiation-resistant dipole dump magnet for MIR/THz FEL beam disposal
key_results: 45 MeV beam deflection at 42° with 18.7°C coil temperature rise under radiation; 99% neutron attenuation via composite shielding; air-cooled operation maintained via parallel winding scheme
comments: Coupled MCNP-COMSOL approach successfully integrates radiation transport and thermal management, demonstrating robust engineering for harsh FEL environments.

model_io.model: C-type dipole dump magnet for TARLA FEL
Coupled MCNP-COMSOL radiation transport and thermal simulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Composite shielding achieves 99% neutron attenuation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by experimental shielding characterization

title: 2D materials assisted terahertz modulators and sensors
task: Review 2D materials for THz modulator and sensor applications
key_results: Graphene, TMDs, MXenes enable subwavelength phase/amplitude/polarization control for THz; comprehensive device mechanisms identified; key challenges in integration and tunability addressed
comments: Timely survey consolidating emerging 2D-material THz devices, though practical integration challenges and noise performance still limit deployment.

title: Progress and prospects in the underground laboratories' search for dark matter
task: Review dark matter search progress in underground laboratories
key_results: Multiple complementary detection technologies developed to explore diverse dark matter parameter space; key improvements needed for future detector sensitivity
comments: Comprehensive perspective on experimental status but lacks quantitative projections for discovery reach beyond current constraints.

title: Collective dynamics of polar nanoregions enhance electrical properties via solid-solution-induced entropy increase in KBT relaxors
task: Understand collective PNR dynamics and jamming effects in relaxor ferroelectrics
key_results: Self-assembled 10-1000 nm mesostructures from 2-4 nm polar nanoregions via Turing instability; jamming effects within mesostructures enhance electrostrain and energy storage by entropy-driven disorder
comments: Reveals nanoscale self-organization mechanism universal to relaxors through Turing patterns, providing design principles for high-performance ferroelectrics.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PNR mesostructures arise from Turing instability
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by Gray-Scott model matching experimental mesostructure patterns

title: Mean field magnetism and spin frustration in a double perovskite oxide with compositional complexity
task: Investigate magnetic ordering in high-entropy double perovskite oxide
key_results: Curie temperature ~150 K maintained despite compositional disorder and high cationic variance; reentrant spin-glass behavior at low T with mean-field capturing long-range transition
comments: Shows entropy-driven disorder can preserve ferromagnetism while introducing spin-glass physics at low temperatures, offering new design principles for functional oxides.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Phonon renormalization follows mean-field spin-correlation below Tc
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by Raman spectroscopy agreement with mean-field model

title: Signed, sealed, delivered: a generalizable model for living biotherapeutic dosing and metabolism
task: Optimize living biotherapeutic probiotic dosing for microbiome-mediated metabolic disorders
key_results: BCAT model achieves 5% mean prediction error vs. ~30% for prior models; predicts ~10⁹ CFU dosed 3–4 h before meals achieves 95% trimethylamine reduction in trimethylaminuria
comments: The mechanistic coupling of transit, metabolism, and enzyme kinetics is rigorous, though clinical validation is limited to two datasets and enzyme expression remains the dominant parameter—suggesting potential brittleness in generalization.

model_io.model: BCAT (Bacterial Compartment Absorption and Transit) pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic model

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mechanistic coupling of probiotic transit and enzymatic metabolism improves prediction over compartmental models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 5% vs. 30% error; validated against native choline and SYNB1618 clinical trials

hypotheses.hypothesis: Enzyme expression level dominates sensitivity; dosing time and dose are secondary parameters
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: global sensitivity analysis identifies enzyme expression as dominant design parameter
